retry_reason: Put operations are unconditional (aside from null check) and always succeed; retrying is not applicable.
contract_summary: This function releases the AppArmor label references held by a task context (the nnp, previous, and onexec fields). It is a cleanup helper that should be called when discarding a task_ctx structure. After calling aa_free_task_ctx, the labels inside the context are no longer valid and must not be accessed, though the context struct itself can be freed. It is the put-equivalent for a set of fields, analogous to a destructor. The caller must ensure that the context is not used again after this call without re-initializing the fields.

judge.reason: All the function does is conditionally call aa_put_label on three fields of the context; no other logic. It is a pure multi-put wrapper for releasing references on label objects.

check.reason: The function aa_free_task_ctx calls aa_put_label on ctx->nnp, ctx->previous, and ctx->onexec. Each call decrements the refcount of the label via the path count.count.refcount, consistent with the callee info. The access paths start from parameter 1 (ctx) and follow the struct fields to the refcount. The type chains correctly include aa_task_ctx followed by the callee's chain. Segment counts match (5 each). All operations are put operations as expected.
